command: result = [{EXPRESSION} for item in iterable if {CONDITION}]
title: List comprehension with condition
description: Python's elegant way to build lists. Filter and transform in one line.

command: result = {k: v for k, v in iterable.items() if {CONDITION}}
title: Dict comprehension
description: Build a dict in one line. Like list comprehension but for dictionaries.

command: python -m venv {ENV_NAME} && source {ENV_NAME}/bin/activate
title: Create and activate virtual env
description: Isolate your project's packages. Always use a venv. Always.

command: pip install {PACKAGE}
title: Install Python package
description: Install a package from PyPI. Are you in your venv? You should be.

command: pip freeze > requirements.txt
title: Export dependencies to requirements.txt
description: Freeze your current environment to a file. Share it so others can reproduce.

command: pip install -r requirements.txt
title: Install from requirements.txt
description: Reproduce someone's environment. The first step after cloning a repo.

command:
with open('{FILENAME}', 'r') as f:
    content = f.read()
title: Read file contents
description: The Pythonic way to read a file. Context manager ensures the file is always closed.

command:
with open('{FILENAME}', 'w') as f:
    f.write('{CONTENT}')
title: Write to file
description: Write content to a file. 'w' overwrites, 'a' appends. Know the difference.

command:
import json
with open('{FILE}.json') as f:
    data = json.load(f)
title: Load JSON from file
description: Parse a JSON file into a Python dict. The universal data format.

command:
import json
with open('{FILE}.json', 'w') as f:
    json.dump(data, f, indent=2)
title: Write dict to JSON file
description: Serialize a Python object to JSON. indent=2 makes it human-readable.

title: Define a dataclass
description: Python's built-in data container with auto-generated __init__, __repr__, __eq__.

title: Create a function decorator
description: The decorator template with @wraps to preserve the function's metadata.

command:
def process(items: list[str], limit: int = 10) -> dict[str, int]:
    ...
title: Function with type hints
description: Modern Python type annotations. Better IDE support, fewer bugs, more readable.

command: for index, value in enumerate(iterable):
title: Loop with index and value
description: Get the index AND the value in a for loop. No more range(len(x)).

command: for a, b in zip(list1, list2):
title: Loop over two lists simultaneously
description: Pair up two iterables and loop over them together. Pythonic parallel iteration.

command:
import argparse
parser = argparse.ArgumentParser()
parser.add_argument('--{NAME}', type=str, required=True)
args = parser.parse_args()
title: Parse command-line arguments
description: Built-in argument parsing. Your CLI scripts deserve proper argument handling.

command:
import urllib.request
import json

with urllib.request.urlopen('{URL}') as response:
    data = json.loads(response.read())
title: HTTP GET with stdlib (no requests)
description: Make HTTP calls without installing requests. urllib is always available.

command:
import requests
response = requests.get('{URL}', headers={'Authorization': 'Bearer {TOKEN}'})
data = response.json()
title: HTTP GET with requests library
description: The human-friendly HTTP library. Install with pip install requests.

command: msg = f'Hello, {name}! Items: {len(items):,}'
title: f-string with formatting
description: Python's most readable string interpolation. :, formats numbers with commas.

title: Create a generator function
description: Yield values one at a time. Memory-efficient for large datasets.

title: Create a context manager
description: Build your own 'with' statement. Always runs cleanup, even on exceptions.
